[2019-03-26 19:46] mrguy888: Original deadeye was at a time where every single person who played prismata was unbelievably bad. Unless you go back and analyze those games now I don't think you can say you know anything about the unit.
[2019-03-26 19:46] sano712 (sano): How interesting would be a 1-supply deadeye with infinite stamina? Cost subjected to changes, obviously.
[2019-03-26 19:49] sano712 (sano): And that's why it's a bad comparison
[2019-03-26 19:49] mrguy888: A 1 supply deadeye would be better than a 4 supply deadeye just like 4 supply is better than 10 supply. But you gotta ask: if less is better is 1 really better than 0?
[2019-03-26 19:50] sano712 (sano): If 4 supply deadeye is better than 10 supply deadeye, would you gladly play an asymmetric game where you have 4 supply deadeye and you opponent has 10?
[2019-03-26 19:50] mrguy888: Obviously I meant better for the game, not better to have in a gameplay perspective.
[2019-03-26 19:50] sano712 (sano): I'm just joking at this point
[2019-03-26 19:51] sano712 (sano): But the infinite stamina thingy was a real question
[2019-03-26 19:51] mrguy888: My answer was not a joke, I think it would be better than what we have.
[2019-03-26 19:51] sano712 (sano): I'm torn between it would be an interesting unit and it would be an extremely boring unit
[2019-03-26 19:51] mrguy888: But also disagree with the concept of doing that.
[2019-03-26 19:52] mrguy888: Manticore deadeye was the best version of deadeye from a design point, though not from a specific balance point.
[2019-03-26 19:52] mrguy888: But then we got manticore instead, which is a good unit.
[2019-03-26 19:54] jamberine: I'm curious how bad "unbelievably bad" is. What do you think a top player from then would be rated today
[2019-03-26 19:55] mrguy888: Tough to say, but when you look at old top games and read what top players said about the game back then, it is quite laughable.
[2019-03-26 19:55] mrguy888: Elyot was a top 5 player at the time he thought 4rr 10 span brooder was not op, for example.
[2019-03-26 19:56] mrguy888: It isn't like anyone else had better balance opinions at the time, but now we can have a good laugh at that.
[2019-03-26 19:57] mrguy888: Dbelange was known for being an omega clicker despite being a pretty big distance ahead of the pack.
[2019-03-26 19:57] mrguy888: He just thought it was good, it seems.
[2019-03-26 19:58] mrguy888: Top 10 players thought Iso was op.
[2019-03-26 20:00] Deleted User (Deleted User): he was? wow kids sucked
[2019-03-26 20:01] wilson0862 (Wilson): Yeah your current skill might've even been top 20 back then. 😮
[2019-03-26 20:01] mrguy888: VkU2c-gkRzb I just randomly selected a 2015 era spot on my replay list and picked the first top 10 name I saw
[2019-03-26 20:01] mrguy888: dblenage rushed dmech
[2019-03-26 20:01] Deleted User (Deleted User): omega clicking and iso buying is just so shit i can't imagine not owning kids who do those
[2019-03-26 20:02] amalloy: i wonder if elyot just really liked the chill mechanic, and convinced himself it wasn't OP because he wanted to believe it. it seems like a huge number of chill units have been nerfed
[2019-03-26 20:02] Deleted User (Deleted User): probs cause kids sucked using chill maybe
[2019-03-26 20:03] awaclus: We had a lot of players, especially beginners, saying 'Frost brooder is so op! It's crazy!' It's not op. It's just that it's very strong against that specific strategy which is a strategy that beginners learn first when they play Prismata. Obviously Frost Brooder is horrible against Gauss Cannons.
[2019-03-26 20:04] mrguy888: I think it went more like "frostbrooder is op" (rushed 10 bites with 3 swords, opponent defends for 34)
[2019-03-26 20:04] mrguy888: but that loses to 10 swords and no shields
[2019-03-26 20:04] mrguy888: so elyot beat those guys and didn't see the problem
[2019-03-26 20:05] mrguy888: I remember there being a buzz about a game where will won a game after getting lucina breached vs timex who made many brooders
[2019-03-26 20:06] mrguy888: both were top 5 players at the time
[2019-03-26 20:06] Deleted User (Deleted User): would timex be real badkid if he were to re-enter in today's meta?
[2019-03-26 20:06] awaclus: being a real badkid if you were to re-enter in today's meta?
[2019-03-26 20:07] mrguy888: He would be initially, but would be better than you are now in under a month, I assume
[2019-03-26 20:08] jamberine: I'd like to think if I played then I would be able to figure out that iso sucks, for example, but I guess you just learn so much by playing good people
[2019-03-26 20:08] awaclus: most master players can probably become better than cooled is now in under a month if they try hard?
[2019-03-26 20:08] mrguy888: Yeah, it is way easier to catch up than forge ahead in uncharted waters.
[2019-03-26 20:09] mrguy888: Iso specifically is hard because you need to not buy too much or too little defence on the off turn.
[2019-03-26 20:09] awaclus: >I would be able to figure out that iso sucks
not as much as Goko
[2019-03-26 20:10] mrguy888: That is hard for new and bad players
[2019-03-26 20:12] mrguy888: There was even debate on whether or not buying drone and forcefield on the same turn costs 3 or 4 gold, along with the lucina version of the question.
[2019-03-26 20:12] shadourow (Rando): Well...
I'm also pretty sure that I obliterate Pythagoras at Maths
[2019-03-26 20:13] amalloy: @mrguy888 i think there are still a lot of players who'd answer that question wrong
[2019-03-26 20:13] shadourow (Rando): That's just not really useful to compare the what the community knew before with what you know now
[2019-03-26 20:13] mrguy888: That is my point though. Opinions on the original deadeye are meaningless unless backed up by modern analysis
[2019-03-26 20:13] sano712 (sano): I don't know much about old prismata days, but looking at the early Dota 2 days is hilarious
[2019-03-26 20:14] mrguy888: It is the same with any game or any field.
[2019-03-26 20:14] amalloy: i know i'm not the sort of player who can really forge ahead in discovering what's good. i'm okay at applying stuff i hear from top players, but a lot of "my" opinions are just stuff i heard and internalized

[2019-03-26 20:16] sano712 (sano): Isn't the main issue with bombarder the same as Odin? First player to get it gets a big edge because of the immediate threat
[2019-03-26 20:16] sano712 (sano): In that sense, nerfing gold cost by one doesn't change much
[2019-03-26 20:16] mrguy888: No, I have not seen bomber be particularly player favored

[2019-03-26 20:18] shadourow (Rando): That guy has a point :monkaS:

[2019-03-26 20:19] sano712 (sano): I think people dislike that because it leads to boring game play
[2019-03-26 20:20] mrguy888: If it costed less then people wouldn't rush it as hard though.
[2019-03-26 20:20] amalloy: @sano you can get first bombarder on like turn 4, if "first bombarder" is really what wins. DD D BBA 1
[2019-03-26 20:20] amalloy: people don't do that because first bombarder isn't what wins
[2019-03-26 20:21] jamberine: first odin in that sense didn't win either
[2019-03-26 20:21] mrguy888: Yeah when people say first to buy it wins I usually understand their point to be that one player has a reasonable path to get it first and the other player has to do something dumb like dd d bba to get it first. This leads to spots where one player loses by getting it first and loses by getting it second.
[2019-03-26 20:21] sano712 (sano): I didn't say it's an auto win. It's just that first bombarder gets an edge because it makes it hard for opponent to answer with his own bombarder due to the 3 damage that just got added. Obviously, it is important to know the optimal time of buying it. But the point remains  I think
[2019-03-26 20:22] mrguy888: First anything gets an edge though.
[2019-03-26 20:22] mrguy888: First tarsiers, first cynestra, first omega.
[2019-03-26 20:23] mrguy888: The question is always if the first whatever outweighs the value of giving over first absorb.
[2019-03-26 20:24] mrguy888: I think your complaint about getting a lot of bombers after buying 2 or so tarsiers is fixed by the gold nerf though, and not so much by the supply nerf.
[2019-03-26 20:26] mrguy888: You gotta ask how relevant the 5th bomber is in those games. In the mirror the 5th bomber is not relevant at all and against a non mirror you delay the bombers already so I don't know how relevant the 5th bomber is there either.

[2019-03-27 00:40] zera777 (Zera): Is Thermite Core OP? It's really effective at denying absorb.
[2019-03-27 00:43] mrguy888: Seeing as all it does is deny absorb one should hope it does its job. It is less op than bomber, and not player favoring at all but I think the healthiest prismata game has a weaker thermite. It's not really an issue atm.
[2019-03-27 00:44] amalloy: we know it's at least twice as strong as a unit that's unplayably bad
[2019-03-27 00:44] mrguy888: The only unplayable unit in prismata is endo
[2019-03-27 00:44] awaclus: endo is this unit [[Endotherm Kit]]
[2019-03-27 00:45] Deleted User (Deleted User): old thermite is slightly more than half as good as new one
[2019-03-27 00:45] mrguy888: Umm, no it is not more than half as good as this one.
[2019-03-27 00:45] awaclus: it might not even be half as good
[2019-03-27 00:45] mrguy888: Less for sure. There is so much value in having up to 8 damage hidden.
[2019-03-27 00:46] Deleted User (Deleted User): more than twice having up to 4?
[2019-03-27 00:46] mrguy888: The more damage you can hide, the more valuable your first thermites are.
[2019-03-27 00:46] mrguy888: So it is twice as strong as well as more strong than that due to working for longer.
[2019-03-27 00:47] awaclus: if it was 10-supply, then it would probably be closer to half as good
[2019-03-27 00:48] mrguy888: Closer for sure, but there is also some value in tech balancing. Old thermite would require you to own more blastforges, which is really bad if you don't want those forges after the thermiting.
[2019-03-27 00:49] awaclus: the old thermite was pretty much only usable for buying 1 of them with a spare blue in hopes of getting an extra damage somewhere due to opponent lacking granularity (or forcing them to go more granular)
[2019-03-27 00:51] mrguy888: The sad thing about old thermite is that your opponent starts overdefended cause of their starting engis and buying an absorber. The pixies wouldn't get any threat value until you run out of thermites already.
[2019-03-27 00:52] zera777 (Zera): Would thermite be more balanced if it was 3 attack for 2 pixies?
[2019-03-27 00:52] awaclus: that's an enormous nerf
[2019-03-27 00:52] wilson0862 (Wilson): It would be perfectly balanced.
[2019-03-27 00:52] wilson0862 (Wilson): Nobody would ever buy it.
[2019-03-27 00:53] mrguy888: 2b is my favourite nerf or 3bb for a two pack of single thermites 😮
[2019-03-27 00:53] f300xen (f0x): its presence wouldn't change p1/p2 winrates at all
[2019-03-27 00:54] awaclus: pro tip: the next time you consider suggesting nerfing something by making a player get 1 fewer attack per turn, the answer is no
[2019-03-27 00:54] mrguy888: I disagree. I think base+more is more balanced than base+less
[2019-03-27 00:55] Deleted User (Deleted User): what abt 2b for a 2 pack of single thermites
[2019-03-27 00:55] f300xen (f0x): not if the more is 66% thermite
[2019-03-27 00:56] Deleted User (Deleted User): 3bb for a two pack seems real badkid
[2019-03-27 00:56] shadourow (Rando): What about 1.5B thermite ?
[2019-03-27 00:57] mrguy888: i wouldnt be opposed to doubling all the numbers in prismata
[2019-03-27 00:57] shadourow (Rando): As you'll definitely be using the other half gold next turn anyway
[2019-03-27 00:57] mrguy888: and then making some units have odd numbers
[2019-03-27 00:58] shadourow (Rando): Tbh, that idea isn't terrible, but it would be a huge modification for few effects
[2019-03-27 00:58] shadourow (Rando): Also, drones would look terrible
[2019-03-27 00:58] shadourow (Rando): with all their numbers doubled
[2019-03-27 00:59] mrguy888: I think a lot of units would be better with odd numbers in doublemata
[2019-03-27 00:59] Deleted User (Deleted User): have a 2v2. double all the numbers and add the non doubled opponents' attack

[2019-04-03 03:21] apooche: It has been said many times, but a 50/50 winrate does not imply that player-favoring has been eliminated in prismata
[2019-04-03 03:23] f300xen (f0x): 50% of sets are 100% p1 wins and 50% of sets are 100% p2 wins
[2019-04-03 03:23] f300xen (f0x): thus prismata is not player favoring
[2019-04-03 03:24] apooche: a 50/50 winrate in a given set would mean it is pretty balanced. but a 50/50 winrate among all sets just means as many sets favor one side as the other
[2019-04-03 03:25] apooche: the one semi-decent test of overall player favoredness happened a long time ago and involved experienced players placing confidence wagers on a number of sets. the favoredness rating came out to an average of around 55%-60%, while the p2 overall winrate was around 52%
[2019-04-03 03:27] mrguy888: Fundamentally, Prismata is an unfair game. With the extremely rare draws as exception, every set is a forced win for one player or another. In practice, it is complicated enough that the better player wins more frequently.
[2019-04-03 03:27] apooche: I think it has been improved a lot since then, but the test hasn't been repeated. I think Dave's master student is helping develop a learning AI that has a side effect of predicting favoredness, but it sounds like it is a ways off and it would be difficult to train it on actual player game data based on some technical difficulties
[2019-04-03 03:27] mrguy888: I think that test had the issue where the games were unbalanced in terms of the player's ratings.
[2019-04-03 03:27] apooche: yeah but that was hidden during the guessing

messages.content: the one semi-decent test of overall player favoredness happened a long time ago and involved experienced players placing confidence wagers on a number of sets. the favoredness rating came out to an average of around 55%-60%, while the p2 overall winrate was around 52%

messages.author: mrguy888
messages.content: Fundamentally, Prismata is an unfair game. With the extremely rare draws as exception, every set is a forced win for one player or another. In practice, it is complicated enough that the better player wins more frequently.

messages.author: apooche
messages.content: I think it has been improved a lot since then, but the test hasn't been repeated. I think Dave's master student is helping develop a learning AI that has a side effect of predicting favoredness, but it sounds like it is a ways off and it would be difficult to train it on actual player game data based on some technical difficulties

messages.author: mrguy888
messages.content: I think that test had the issue where the games were unbalanced in terms of the player's ratings.

messages.author: apooche
messages.content: yeah but that was hidden during the guessing

messages.author: mrguy888
messages.content: Yeah, but that is a problem.

messages.author: apooche
messages.content: it adds noise. I think doing only 20 rounds made the result pretty weak as well

messages.author: mrguy888
messages.content: Just because a 1600 player lost to a 2000 player doesn't mean the guessers would have still been wrong if it were two 2000 players

messages.author: apooche
messages.content: yeah it just adds a strong noise but it doesn't totally break the result
